text: High-volume sub-elite marathon training plans recommend an average weekly running volume of 108 km during the final 12 weeks before a marathon, with peak week exceeding 90 km.
Middle-volume sub-elite marathon training plans recommend an average weekly running volume of 59 km during the final 12 weeks before a marathon, with peak week between 65-90 km.
Low-volume sub-elite marathon training plans recommend an average weekly running volume of 43 km during the final 12 weeks before a marathon, with peak week below 65 km.
Sub-elite marathon training plans follow a pyramidal intensity distribution, with the largest proportion of training volume in zone 2 (63-67%) across all volume categories.
High-volume marathon plans allocate intensity as 15% zone 1, 67% zone 2, 10% zone 3, 5% zone 4, and 3% zone 5.
Lower-volume marathon plans allocate a higher proportion of training to zone 3 (threshold) intensity (17-18%) compared to high-volume plans (10%), compensating for reduced total volume.
Across all volume categories, sub-elite marathon training plans recommend only 2-5% of training in zone 4 and 2-3% in zone 5, keeping high-intensity work to a small fraction of total volume.
